


Data exists in a range of different places, namely

- Hemsbase
- HEMS Desk
- TARN (Trauma Audit and Research Network) Data
- Ambulance Service

Data from all of these sources may not be available in the first instance of the model.

## Hemsbase

Since clinical independence, Hemsbase has been used as the primary storage location for data by the air ambulance charity.

## HEMS Desk

The HEMS desk data can provide insight into incidents where a critical care (helicopter or critical care car) would have been allocated if they were available.

This can occur in several ways:

- The care resources are already committed on another incident
- the care resource is offiline during operational hours
- the care resource if offline outside of operationaal hours

## TARN Data

This data can provide additional insight into those patients who were not conveyed.

## SWAST

Additional categories of possible incidents could be obtained from the local ambulance service.
